title: Open-End Kitchen Wall Shelf
summary: A small open shelf sized to close off the open end of a cabinet run — a side panel matched to your cabinets, four short shelves, and 1x2 trim finishing every edge.
description:
This shelf exists to solve one specific problem: an open, unfinished end where a cabinet run stops short of a wall. The side panel is cut to match your actual cabinet side panels in both size and material — **use 1x12 or 3/4" plywood, and choose the wood species or finish to match your existing cabinets exactly**, since this piece is meant to look like it was part of the original cabinet run.

Four shelves attach to the side panel at heights that suit what's being displayed or stored, with 1x2 trim capping the front edges. **Every measurement here is explicitly meant to be adjusted to fit your own kitchen** — the plan's numbers are a starting point, not a fixed size, since cabinet heights and depths vary between kitchens.

Mount by screwing into the adjacent cabinet and into wall studs behind — a shelf like this needs to be solid on both connections, since it's visually load-bearing even if it's not holding much weight.
